title: Standard Operating Procedure: Automated Procurement Workflow
content:
STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE: AUTOMATED PROCUREMENT WORKFLOW
Document ID: SOP-PROC-001
Version: 2.0
Effective Date: 2024-03-01
Owner: Procurement Operations Lead

1. PURPOSE
This SOP describes the end-to-end steps of the automated procurement workflow executed by the supply chain AI system. It is intended for operations staff who monitor the system and for auditors reviewing procurement decisions.

2. WORKFLOW OVERVIEW
The automated procurement workflow is triggered when the system detects that on-hand inventory for a tracked material is forecast to fall below safety stock within the next planning horizon (30 days).

Step 1 — Inventory Check: The system retrieves current on-hand quantities and safety stock levels from the SAP inventory module.
Step 2 — Demand Forecast: The system analyses 18 months of shipment history to produce a next-month demand forecast with confidence bounds.
Step 3 — Procurement Proposal: If forecast demand exceeds on-hand stock, the system generates a purchase requisition proposal including recommended quantity, preferred vendor, unit price, and total estimated cost.
Step 4 — Policy Evaluation: The proposal is evaluated against current procurement approval policies (see POL-PROC-001 and POL-PROC-002). The evaluation engine applies rules in the following priority order:
  (a) Forbidden vendor check — deny if vendor is on the Restricted Vendor List.
  (b) Threshold check — apply the approval tier matching the estimated cost.
  (c) Vendor constraint check — verify preferred vendor requirement is met.
Step 5 — Outcome:
  - Auto-approved: a purchase order is created automatically in SAP.
  - Needs human approval: the requisition is placed in the approval queue and the responsible manager is notified.
  - Denied: the workflow terminates and the operations team is notified to intervene manually.

3. APPROVAL QUEUE MANAGEMENT
Approvals pending in the queue for more than 8 business hours are automatically escalated to the next management level. The operations team must review the approval queue dashboard at the start of each business day.

4. THRESHOLDS REFERENCE (as of SOP effective date)
The following thresholds apply to standard catalogue materials:
- Below $5,000 with preferred vendor: auto-approved
- $5,000 to $25,000: manager approval required
- Above $25,000: executive approval required (system denies and routes to CapEx board)

These values are governed by POL-PROC-001. If POL-PROC-001 is updated, this SOP reference table must be updated in the same change cycle.

5. EXCEPTION HANDLING
If the policy evaluation engine cannot extract a numeric threshold from a policy document (ambiguous language), it defaults to routing the requisition for human review. Operations staff should log such exceptions and notify the compliance team to update the policy wording.

6. AUDIT TRAIL
Every automated procurement decision produces a machine-readable audit record containing: material ID, vendor ID, estimated cost, the policy rule that fired, the outcome, and a timestamp. These records are retained in the Cosmos DB audit container for a minimum of 7 years in accordance with the company records retention policy.
